data.title: Curve founder criticizes frequent vulnerabilities in DeFi: single points of failure pose a hidden danger, calls for unified security standards.
data.description: Curve founder Michael Egorov stated in an article on the X platform that DeFi will remain the future of the global financial system, but recent security incidents, many of which could have been avoided (due to centralized single points of failure), are severely damaging the industry's development. He cited the Aave and rsETH incidents as examples, pointing out that while multiple parties claimed the "system was operating normally," users were unable to withdraw funds, exposing infrastructure dependence and issues of dispersed responsibility. Egorov emphasized that the industry should mitigate single points of failure risk beforehand and disperse trust when unavoidable, while sharing best practices and security experiences. He called for the establishment of unified DeFi security standards and suggested that ecosystem organizations, including the Ethereum Foundation and the Solana Foundation, take the lead in developing principles and standards for building and verifying security.

data.title: North Huachuang Addresses Pricing Strategy Amid Rising Raw Material Costs
data.description: North Huachuang announced on April 21 via its interactive platform that the company sets prices based on market and customer demand. According to Jin10, in response to rising raw material costs, North Huachuang plans to mitigate cost pressures through supply chain optimization and technological innovation. The company currently does not anticipate any price increases.

data.title: Hong Kong Stocks Open Higher with Gains in Hang Seng Index
data.description: Hong Kong's stock market opened with positive momentum as the Hang Seng Index rose by 0.31% at the start of trading. According to Jin10, the Hang Seng Tech Index also saw an increase, opening up by 0.53%. These early gains indicate a positive start for the market, reflecting investor optimism in the region.

data.title: A newly created wallet withdrew 199,999 HYPE from a CEX and sold them for 8.15 million USDC.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a newly created wallet withdrew 199,999 HYPE from a CEX and sold them for 8.15 million USDC. The wallet then withdrew the funds from Hyperliquid and deposited them into Binance.

data.title: Hong Kong-based crypto payment company adds support for Sui and native USDC.
data.description: Hong Kong-based crypto payment company RedotPay announced the addition of SUI and USDC-SUI support to its app, enabling users to make purchases and transfers in over 100 countries worldwide using its cards and payment services. This integration, based on the Sui network developed by Mysten Labs, enables support for native USDC, marking an expansion from cross-chain assets to native assets. RedotPay stated that it currently has over 7 million users and an annualized payment volume exceeding $10 billion. (TechinAsia)

data.title: Analysis: Attention should be paid to the progress of US-Iran negotiations and the resumption of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz. Silver is expected to follow gold's upward trend in the short term, with volatility potentially exceeding that of gold.
data.description: Silver prices were affected by the renewed blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, the escalating US-Iran conflict, and inflation concerns. Silver's industrial properties make it more sensitive to global growth and risk appetite, resulting in greater volatility than gold. The extent of the US economic stagnation remains unproven, and the renewed escalation of US-Iran geopolitical tensions has led to a rise in oil prices today. Short-term interest rate cut expectations are unlikely to rise rapidly, and further economic data is needed for guidance. Furthermore, the spot market for silver remains weak, with 1-month, 3-month, and 6-month London silver leasing rates, while slightly higher, still fluctuating at low levels. Short-term demand expectations for silver from the industrial sector are also weak. The cancellation of photovoltaic tax rebates starting in April may slow down the previous rush to export silver from the photovoltaic sector. (Jinshi)

data.title: Grayscale revises Hyperliquid ETF application: Changes custodian to Anchorage Digital Bank, removes Coinbase.
data.description: Grayscale has updated its filing for its Hyperliquid-linked ETF, changing the custodian to Anchorage Digital Bank, replacing Coinbase as the prime broker and custodian. This change is noteworthy because Coinbase has long dominated the crypto ETF custody market, currently providing custody services for almost all US spot Bitcoin ETFs (except Fidelity). The filing shows that Bank of New York Mellon will remain the transfer agent for the ETF (proposed ticker symbol GHYP). The fund's staking functionality still requires regulatory approval and will use Hyperliquid benchmark pricing data from CoinDesk. Furthermore, Anchorage Digital Bank, as the first federally chartered crypto bank in the US, has been continuously expanding its institutional service capabilities in recent years, including stablecoins, wealth management, and token lifecycle management. (The Block)

data.title: RedotPay Expands Support for SUI and USDC-Sui
data.description:
Hong Kong-based crypto payment company RedotPay has announced the addition of SUI and USDC-Sui support in its application. According to Odaily, this integration allows users to conduct transactions and make payments in over 100 countries using its card and payment services. The integration is based on the Sui network developed by Mysten Labs, marking a shift from cross-chain assets to native assets.

RedotPay reports having over 7 million users and an annual payment volume exceeding $10 billion.

data.title: South Korea's Central Bank Governor Advocates for Cautious Monetary Policy
data.description: South Korea's central bank governor has emphasized the importance of pursuing inflation stability and financial stability through cautious and flexible monetary policy operations. According to Jin10, the governor highlighted the need for careful management of monetary policy to address economic challenges and ensure stability in the financial sector. The approach aims to balance inflation control with the broader goal of maintaining financial stability in the country.

data.title: XRP Spot ETFs See Significant Daily Inflows
data.description: XRP spot ETFs experienced a notable daily net inflow of $2.997 million, according to ChainCatcher, citing data from SoSoValue. The Grayscale XRP Trust ETF (GXRP) led with a daily net inflow of $2.2198 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $121 million. Following this, the Franklin XRP ETF (XRPZ) recorded a daily net inflow of $777,100, with a historical total net inflow reaching $345 million. As of the latest update, the total net asset value of XRP spot ETFs stands at $1.076 billion, with an XRP net asset ratio of 1.22%. The cumulative historical net inflow has reached $1.278 billion.

data.title: Aave disclosed bad debt data: depending on the different rsETH accounting scheme, there could be two possible bad debt scenarios: $123.7 million and $230.1 million.
data.description: Aave officials stated regarding the bad debts arising from the rsETH theft that there are two possible scenarios depending on how rsETH is handled. If the stolen losses are deducted from all circulating rsETH, the estimated bad debt is $123.7 million, concentrated on the Ethereum mainnet in absolute terms, but most severely affected on Mantle. If the value of rsETH on the mainnet is preserved, and the losses are entirely recorded in the Layer 2 mapped version of rsETH, the estimated bad debt is $230.1 million, all located on Layer 2. Mantle will face a 71.45% WETH shortfall, and Arbitrum will face a 26.67% shortfall; the Ethereum mainnet will be unaffected. Aave added that the final outcome depends on decisions beyond Aave's control, primarily the rsETH accounting method and the LRTOracle's exchange rate update method.

data.title: The US SOL spot ETF saw a total net inflow of $3.2838 million in a single day.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, yesterday (April 20th, Eastern Time), the total net inflow of SOL spot ETFs was $3.2838 million. The SOL spot ETF with the largest net inflow yesterday was the Fidelity Solana Fund ETF (FSOL), with a net inflow of $2.5425 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $158 million. The second largest was the VanEck Solana ETF (VSOL), with a net inflow of $568,600, bringing its historical total net inflow to $19.1098 million. As of press time, the total net asset value of SOL spot ETFs was $872 million, with an equity ratio of 1.76%, and a cumulative historical net inflow of $1.013 billion.

data.title: US stocks closed mixed in the crypto sector, with Hut 8 rising over 5%.
data.description: According to data from msx.com, at the close of trading on the US stock market, the Dow Jones Industrial Average initially closed down 0.01%, the S&P 500 fell 0.24%, and the Nasdaq Composite fell 0.26%. The crypto sector was mixed, with CRCL rising 0.42%, Coinbase rising 2.57%, Strategy rising 2.58%, Hut 8 rising 5.46%, and ALT5 falling 5.53%. msx.com is a decentralized RWA trading platform that has listed hundreds of RWA tokens, covering US stock and ETF tokens such as AAPL, AMZN, GOOGL, META, MSFT, NFLX, and NVDA.

data.title: US media: Vance will attend the meeting; the Revolutionary Guard demands that Iran take a tough stance.
data.description: Three sources said Vance is expected to depart for Islamabad Tuesday morning to negotiate a potential deal with Iran to end the war. While reaching a comprehensive agreement under such a tight timetable is extremely challenging, Trump may agree to extend the deadline if signs of progress emerge. The White House spent Monday awaiting a signal from Tehran confirming it would send a negotiating delegation to Islamabad. One source revealed that Iran had been stalling, with the Iranian Revolutionary Guard apparently pressuring the negotiators to maintain a tougher stance—no negotiations without an end to the US blockade. The Iranian delegation had been waiting for the Supreme Leader's green light. According to sources, approval was granted Monday evening local time. Two sources said Vance would depart Tuesday morning local time, while a third source indicated he might leave late Monday night. (Jinshi)

data.title: Warsh's Nomination Hearing Set for Tuesday
data.description: The U.S. Senate Banking Committee has scheduled the confirmation hearing for Warsh's nomination at 10:00 AM EST on Tuesday. According to NS3.AI, this hearing presents the first substantial opportunity to assess Warsh's position. Adam Button noted the significance of this event, while Politico reported that Warsh's testimony was leaked prior to the session.

data.title: C1 Fund Exits Ripple Investment with 150% Return in Four Months
data.description: C1 Fund has announced a partial exit from its investment in Ripple, achieving an estimated 150% return in under four months. According to NS3.AI, the fund did not reveal the exact profit amount but expressed its intention to continue investing in high-quality cryptocurrency companies. This move reflects C1 Fund's strategic approach to capitalizing on lucrative opportunities within the crypto market.

data.title: Kelp: The reason for the theft was that LayerZero's RPC nodes were compromised, and the 1/1 DVN configuration was set to LayerZero's default settings.
data.description: Kelp DAO officially addressed the theft incident in a post on X, stating that the theft was caused by the compromise of two RPC nodes hosted by LayerZero, while a third RPC node suffered a DDoS attack. This was an attack targeting LayerZero infrastructure; Kelp's own system was not involved in the construction or operation of this infrastructure. The 1/1 DVN configuration is the solution documented in LayerZero's documentation and is the default setting for all new OFT deployments. Kelp has been running on LayerZero infrastructure since January 2024 and has maintained open communication with the LayerZero team. During Kelp's expansion to Layer2, the DVN configuration issue was discussed, and the default configuration was clearly confirmed to be appropriate at that time. Kelp's current priority is to protect user interests and prevent the spread of risks within the DeFi ecosystem. The team is collaborating with various parties within the ecosystem to analyze the impact, garner support, and explore all possible mitigation solutions.

data.title: Altcoin Season Index Remains Steady at 38
data.description: CoinMarketCap's Altcoin Season Index is currently at 38, showing no change from the previous day. According to NS3.AI, this index measures the performance of the top 100 cryptocurrencies, excluding stablecoins and wrapped tokens, against Bitcoin over a 90-day period.

data.title: A certain whale sold 497.73 ETH and bought 3.845 billion ASTEROID in the past two days.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a whale sold 497.73 ETH and bought 3.845 billion ASTEROID in the past two days, with an average purchase price of $0.00029. The whale still holds 369.28 ETH and may continue to buy.

data.title: Bloomberg: Market forecasts are pushing for increased lobbying in Washington to cope with tightening regulations.
data.description: As the U.S. Congress considers stronger regulations, the prediction market industry is intensifying its lobbying efforts in Washington in response to criticism that it fuels gambling expansion and poses potential insider trading risks. Leading platforms, such as Kalshi, along with several cryptocurrency and sports betting companies involved in prediction markets, are forming lobbying teams to influence the legislative process and ease regulatory pressure. Industry insiders worry that regulatory action against this multi-billion dollar sector may accelerate. (Bloomberg)
